headline: Dawkins Cracks Top 100, Practice Tickets Vanish Fast
writeup: Buffalo Bills left tackle Dion Dawkins headlined the team's NFL Top 100 Players of 2026 presence, landing at No. 57 while a key teammate dropped 15 spots on the same list, as the Bills community buzzed with pre-camp excitement punctuated by open practice tickets selling out in under 20 minutes. On the field, training camp storylines are piling up fast — from the wide receiver battle featuring questions about Joshua Palmer's roster spot and a predicted bust at the position, to the post-Milano inside linebacker depth concerns and rookie OLB T.J. Parker's projections drawing historical comparisons. The Bills' injury landscape, offensive line health, and quarterback Kyle Allen's scouting profile are all under the microscope as Joe Brady takes the helm, with first-read targets and breakout candidates emerging as key metrics to watch under the new head coach. Off the gridiron, the Bills and Sabres are teaming up for a Pet Adoption Event on July 25, the team is offering CPR training to the community, and defensive coordinator Jim Leonhard earned recognition among the NFL's top breakout assistant coaches heading into the season. With DJ Moore addition drawing praise, cap space debates swirling, trade rumors linking Buffalo to a Panthers receiver, and AFC odds placing the Bills among the favorites, the franchise enters camp with high stakes, plenty of roster decisions, and a fanbase hungry for a Super Bowl breakthrough.
